skill.name: creativity
skill.description: To transform raw data and abstract problems into novel, feasible, and high-impact creative solutions using analogical "mastication" and multi-stage refinement.
skill.summary: To transform raw data and abstract problems into novel, feasible, and high-impact creative solutions...
skill.author: joeloffbeat

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3. This is a false positive because line 3 is front matter description text, not cryptographic code. No prompt injection, scripts, network access, command execution, or secret handling were found.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static finding at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive. The line is a natural-language description of creative problem solving and contains no cryptographic algorithm or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is front matter description text. No cryptographic code, scripts, commands, network calls, or secret handling appear in the scanned file.

content.value_statement: Creative work often stalls when ideas stay too close to the original problem. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through analogical thinking, synthesis, and reality checks.
actual_capabilities: Breaks a request into purpose, process, and expected output.
Reframes problems around their functional essence.
Uses distant analogies to generate unexpected solution directions.
Synthesizes ideas into concrete prototype narratives.
Reviews concepts for feasibility, utility, and clarity.
Simulates creative sub-roles for pattern finding, reflection, and storytelling.
limitations: Does not validate ideas with external market or user research.
Does not include scripts, automation, or executable workflows.
Long-term memory depends on separate memory tooling being available.
Output quality depends on clear context from the user.

use_cases.title: Reframe a Product Challenge
use_cases.description: Turn a vague product problem into clear creative directions and testable concept narratives.

use_cases.title: Develop Campaign Ideas
use_cases.description: Convert a marketing goal into memorable concepts that use analogy, story, and feasibility checks.

use_cases.title: Explore Technical Design Options
use_cases.description: Use cross-domain analogies to find new ways to structure a system, workflow, or data problem.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify the Creative Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for defining the problem before ideation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the creativity skill to break this request into Why, How, and What. Then identify the functional essence: [describe request].

prompt_templates.title: Generate Analogical Directions
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for expanding beyond obvious ideas.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply analogical churning to this problem. Compare it with biological, mechanical, and historical systems, then propose five concept directions: [problem].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Prototype Narrative
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for turning ideas into a usable concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Choose the strongest analogy and synthesize it into a prototype narrative. Include the audience, core behavior, expected value, and why it works.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Refine the Concept
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for testing practicality and improving the result.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the reality audit on this concept. Score feasibility and utility, identify failure points, and revise it into a more concrete proposal.

output_examples.input: A team needs a more engaging onboarding flow for a complex analytics product.
output_examples.output: The skill would reframe onboarding as trust building, compare it with guided apprenticeship, and propose a staged product tour with proof moments.

output_examples.input: A developer wants new ways to reduce alert fatigue in an operations dashboard.
output_examples.output: The skill would map the issue to signal filtering, explore analogies from air traffic control, and suggest prioritized alert lanes.

output_examples.input: A writer needs a stronger concept for a sustainability article series.
output_examples.output: The skill would identify the core tension, generate cross-domain analogies, and shape a story-driven editorial frame.

best_practices: Provide the audience, goal, constraints, and current idea quality.
Ask for both divergent analogies and a final convergent recommendation.
Use the reality audit before committing to an idea.
anti_patterns: Requesting only a list of random ideas without context.
Skipping feasibility review after choosing an exciting concept.
Using the skill as a substitute for user research or expert validation.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps turn abstract problems into creative concepts using structured reframing, analogies, synthesis, and review.

faq.question: Does it run code or install tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill is instructional and contains no scripts, commands, or automation files.

faq.question: Can it support product strategy work?
faq.answer: Yes. It can reframe product problems, generate concept directions, and review ideas for feasibility.

faq.question: Can it help with writing and campaigns?
faq.answer: Yes. It can shape ideas into story-driven concepts for articles, launches, campaigns, and presentations.

faq.question: Does it store information between sessions?
faq.answer: Only if separate memory tooling is available and configured by the user.

faq.question: What inputs improve results?
faq.answer: Clear goals, target audience, constraints, examples, and success criteria improve the quality of the output.
